revenue_by_merchant.sql:
SELECT m.merchant_name,
       SUM(t.amount) as total_revenue,
       COUNT(*) as txn_count
FROM fact_transactions t, dim_merchant m
WHERE t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id
AND t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01'
GROUP BY m.merchant_name
ORDER BY total_revenue
LIMIT 10;

issues.title: Incorrect revenue calculation
issues.problem: Revenue should only include transactions with status 'COMPLETED'.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue

issues.title: Implicit join syntax
issues.problem: Using implicit join syntax can lead to confusion and errors.
issues.fix: FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Lack of comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining its purpose and logic.
issues.fix: Add comments explaining the purpose of each clause and calculation.

review.corrected_sql: SELECT m.merchant_name, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as total_revenue, COUNT(*) as txn_count FROM fact_transactions t JOIN dim_merchant m ON t.merchant_id = m.merchant_id WHERE t.transaction_date > '2024-01-01' GROUP BY m.merchant_name ORDER BY total_revenue LIMIT 10;
review.summary: The query has a critical correctness issue with revenue calculation and uses an implicit join syntax. It also lacks comments for readability.

customer_spend.sql:
SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier,
       SUM(t.amount) as lifetime_value,
       COUNT(t.transaction_id) as total_orders
FROM dim_customer c
LEFT JOIN fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id
WHERE t.status = 'COMPLETED'
GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email
HAVING SUM(t.amount) > 1000
ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;

issues.title: Missing Join with Merchant Table
issues.problem: The query does not join with the dim_merchant table to get merchant names, which violates the business rule.
issues.fix: LEFT JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant m ON c.merchant_id = m.merchant_id

issues.title: Incorrect Aggregation
issues.problem: The SUM(t.amount) should only include transactions with status 'COMPLETED', but the WHERE clause is filtering after the aggregation.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

issues.title: Lack of Comments
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the purpose and logic of the query.
issues.fix: -- Calculate lifetime value and total orders for customers with lifetime value over $1000

issues.title: Inconsistent Naming
issues.problem: The alias 't' is used for the fact_transactions table, which is not descriptive.
issues.fix: SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value

review.corrected_sql: SELECT c.customer_name, c.email, c.tier, LEFT JOIN sigma_analytics.dim_merchant m ON c.merchant_id = m.merchant_id, SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) as lifetime_value, COUNT(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.transaction_id ELSE NULL END) as total_orders FROM sigma_analytics.dim_customer c LEFT JOIN sigma_analytics.fact_transactions t ON c.customer_id = t.customer_id GROUP BY c.customer_name, c.email HAVING SUM(CASE WHEN t.status = 'COMPLETED' THEN t.amount ELSE 0 END) > 1000 ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC;
review.summary: The query has critical correctness issues due to missing joins and incorrect aggregation. Readability can be improved with comments and consistent naming.

daily_failure_rate.sql:
SELECT transaction_date,
       (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2
        WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED'
        AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date) as failed_count,
       COUNT(*) as total_count,
       failed_count / total_count * 100 as failure_rate
FROM fact_transactions t
GROUP BY transaction_date
ORDER BY transaction_date;

issues.title: Incorrect calculation of failure rate
issues.problem: The failure rate is being calculated incorrectly because failed_count is not defined in the SELECT clause but used in the calculation.
issues.fix: Use the subquery result for failed_count in the calculation.

issues.title: Potential performance issue with correlated subquery
issues.line: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM fact_transactions f2 WHERE f2.status = 'FAILED' AND f2.transaction_date = t.transaction_date
issues.problem: The correlated subquery can be inefficient as it runs for each row in the outer query.
issues.fix: Use a JOIN to avoid the correlated subquery.

issues.title: Lack of comments on complex logic
issues.problem: The query lacks comments explaining the logic, especially around the failure rate calculation.
issues.fix: Add comments to explain the logic.

review.corrected_sql:
WITH failed_transactions AS (SELECT transaction_date, COUNT(*) AS failed_count FROM fact_transactions WHERE status = 'FAILED' GROUP BY transaction_date)
SELECT t.transaction_date,
       COALESCE(ft.failed_count, 0) AS failed_count,
       COUNT(*) AS total_count,
       COALESCE(ft.failed_count, 0) * 100.0 / COUNT(*) AS failure_rate
FROM fact_transactions t
LEFT JOIN failed_transactions ft ON t.transaction_date = ft.transaction_date
GROUP BY t.transaction_date, COALESCE(ft.failed_count, 0)
ORDER BY t.transaction_date;
review.summary: The query had a critical correctness issue with the failure rate calculation and a performance issue with the correlated subquery. Readability was also improved by adding comments.
